interactions.summary: Jiang closes by inviting Dugin's view on world coalescence against Greater North America.

Dugin says Trump may be both mad and useful to a deeper state that wants naked Western power, oil access, global war, and prolonged hegemony, while Russia has grown stronger from Ukraine and accepts multipolarity but has revised its view of Trump after Iran, Gaza, and Epstein denial.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization labels are not reliable in this multi-speaker interview; speaker attribution is inferred from conversational role and named address.

claims.claim: Dugin suggests Trump may be a weapon selected by a deeper Western state to make radical moves, then be sacrificed along with MAGA after the strategic situation changes.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says the deeper-state rationale would be to preserve Western hegemony by burning the Middle East and breaking ties among Russia, Europe, and China.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin agrees with Jiang that the Ukraine war strengthened Russia by recentering its economy, sovereignty, industry, and technological self-reliance.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Russia no longer wants empire but a multipolar order with Russian, Chinese, Indian, Islamic, and Western poles, provided the West abandons supremacy.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

claims.claim: Dugin says Russia initially hoped Trump would focus America on its own hemisphere, but revised that view after Trump denied the Epstein list, backed Gaza, kept supporting Ukraine, and opened new war fronts.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim made in this April 13, 2026 interview.

signature_moments.moment: Trump becomes the mad tool who can expose naked Western power and then be sacrificed.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is Dugin's dramatic answer to Jiang's Greater North America theory.

signature_moments.moment: Ukraine is reframed as Russia's resurrection of technological sovereignty.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It echoes prior Jiang claims that the war trained and strengthened Russia.

signature_moments.moment: Multipolarity turns an enemy into a partner the instant the West drops supremacy.
signature_moments.source_phrase: if someone in the West could accept multi-polarity
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives Dugin's constructive alternative to the Antichrist frame.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Dugin's term for a level beneath the liberal deep state that may use Trump to preserve Western hegemony.
A world order where Russia accepts other civilizational poles and rejects only Western supremacy.

uncertainty_notes.note: Segment 0069/0070 contains rejected ASR boundary damage around “helped to us to”; semantic extraction should read the intended point as the Ukraine war helped Russia recenter itself.
